name: lsp
tagline: Thin reference skill that wraps Codex's LSP MCP tools (diagnostics, definitions, references, rename checks).
verdict: Clean and low-risk: no credential access, no network egress, no shell execution, nothing destructive, no hidden or manipulative instructions. It even explicitly warns the model that lsp.* tool names are MCP calls, not shell commands, cutting off a shell-injection misread. Only minor polish gaps: no explicit negative-use guidance, no stated output contract, a

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do not use when...' guidance (e.g., non-LSP-supported languages, missing servers beyond the lsp.status hint) — minor omission, low practical impact given the narrow scope.
Does not specify the output format/shape returned by each tool call (e.g., what a diagnostics or symbols response looks like), leaving that implicit.
No version field or changelog signal in the frontmatter or body to track skill revisions over time.
